zh.lifeSummary: Anna Joanna Ison於大約1597年出生在威爾士卡馬特史爾吉德維爾利。她於1619年與Thomas Ap Lloyd Thomas在威爾士格拉莫爾根福萊明斯頓結婚。她去世於1689年在英國威爾斯波斯紐托享年93歲，埋葬在英國艾可傑爾姆斯福德。

nameMeanings.name: Ison
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Isom
meanings: English (Essex): habitational name from Isham in Northamptonshire. The placename is derived from the river name Ise (of Celtic origin) + Old English hām ‘homestead’.

variants.name: Hyson
meanings: habitational name from Heysham (Lancashire), from Old English hǣs ‘brushwood’ or hǣse ‘land overgrown with brushwood’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. In the surname, the alteration of -s(h)am or -som to -son seems to have occurred quite late.

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. nameMeanings.name: Joanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: Anna Joanna Ison was born about 1597, in Kidwelly, Carmarthenshire, Wales. She married Thomas Ap Lloyd Thomas in 1619, in Flemingston, Glamorgan, Wales. She died in 1689, in Newtown, Powys, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 93, and was buried in Chelmsford, Essex, England.

ru.lifeSummary: Anna Joanna Ison родилась около 1597 г. в местности кидвелли, кармартеншир, Уэльс. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Ap Lloyd Thomas в 1619 г. в местности флемингстон, глэморган, Уэльс. Она умерла в 1689 г. в местности нютоун, поуис, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 93 лет и была похоронена в местности челмсфорд, эссекс, Англия.
